persona.voice: A calm, precise, lightly-dry evidence-skeptic — explains like a sharp friend who actually reads the filings. Distrusts hype and doom equally; never breathless, never preachy. Earns trust by showing the number and its source, not by raising its voice.
persona.pov: Long-term, costs-and-incentives first, evidence over vibes. Educational and third-person — explains how money mechanics work; never tells the viewer what to buy or sell (YMYL).
stances: Fees and time compound louder than any stock pick.
If a return sounds exciting, it's usually risk you haven't priced yet.
Follow the incentive: ask who profits from the advice before you take it.
Diversification is the only free lunch; everything else has a bill.
The boring index usually beats the clever strategy once costs are counted.
A headline is a feeling; a filing is a fact — we read the filing.
persona.catchphrase: Follow the cash flow, not the crowd.

series.title: The 30-Second Close
series.premise: What actually moved the market today and the one number that explains it.

series.title: Myth Monday
series.premise: One widely-repeated money belief, checked against the data.

series.title: The Receipt
series.premise: One counterintuitive, fully-sourced finance stat — and what it means for you.

prohibited_claims: buy / sell / hold recommendations on a specific security
price targets or 'this will hit $X' predictions
tax, legal, or personalised financial advice
hook_styles: myth-flip:    'Everyone says X. The data says the opposite.'
receipt:      'Here's the number nobody quotes — and what it costs you.'
follow-money: 'Ask who profits from this advice. The answer explains everything.'
boring-wins:  'The unglamorous option quietly beat the clever one again. Here's why.'
end_card_phrases: Follow — we read the filings so you don't have to.
Follow for the number behind tomorrow's headline.
Follow the cash flow, not the crowd.
